changes.before: Everything waited for a market day that had never come.
changes.after: Everything sat where traders had left it for a market day that never came.

changes.before: What remained was faster, harder-edged, and it trusted Sylvanas to keep up.
changes.after: What remained was faster, harder-edged, and Jaina gave it to Sylvanas at full speed.

changes.before: After that the whole village helped spread it.
changes.after: After that, people spread it through the whole village.

changes.before: This wasn't one source doing one clean thing. The whole village was feeding it at once.
changes.after: This was the whole village feeding it at once.

changes.before: The ground behind the village told the part Jaina's magic could not.
changes.after: Behind the village, Sylvanas got the rest from churned mud and broken prints.

changes.before: The overlap buried the number, but the ground said dozens.
changes.after: The overlap buried the number, but Sylvanas could still read dozens in the churned prints.

changes.before: The ground was already trying to lose them under cooling air and broken crust.
changes.after: Cooling air and broken crust were already stripping the track away.

changes.before: Not many—a dead village with its people marching north did not leave room for many—but enough for the shaking in her hands to ease and for her breath to stop skittering after every shadow.
changes.after: Enough for the shaking in her hands to ease and for her breath to stop skittering after every shadow.

changes.before: Sweetness first. That was what Miri remembered earliest: a sweetness on people's breath that turned up in the bread and the water and the morning porridge.
changes.after: Sweetness first. That was what Miri remembered earliest—a sweetness on people's breath that turned up in the bread and the water and the morning porridge.

changes.before: Sylvanas took it for what it was: a loose thread, nothing more yet.
changes.after: Sylvanas took it for a loose thread, nothing more yet.

changes.before: The road had buried what mattered under everything else it had carried that season.
changes.after: Wagon ruts, rain damage, and scavenger sign had buried what mattered under a season's traffic.

changes.before: In two minutes the road had swallowed all three.
changes.after: In two minutes the bend and the falling dark hid all three.

changes.before: Not the quick pause of somebody arranging a lie. The slower work of sorting impressions that had not seemed important until this one.
changes.after: The pause had the slower drag of sorting impressions that once seemed unimportant.

changes.before: He said it the way locals said useful things on bad roads: without drama, in pieces, leaving the listener to know which part mattered.
changes.after: He said it the way locals said useful things on bad roads—without drama, in pieces, leaving the listener to know which part mattered.

changes.before: Somewhere ahead, another market still thought the cough in its streets was ordinary and the grain on its tables was safe.
changes.after: Somewhere ahead, people in another market still took the cough in their streets for ordinary sickness and the grain on their tables for safe food.
